title: Micron announces exit from Crucial consumer business; shipments through end of fiscal Q2
Crucial consumer shipments end (Micron fiscal Q2 close)
Google TurboQuant: 3-bit KV-cache quantization, ~6x memory reduction; memory equities and DDR5 spot sentiment hit
MU -13% single day (Broadcom miss + rates); MUU -26.65%
Micron FQ3-26 earnings; RAM (2x DRAM ETF) launches same day
Korea June exports first-ever $100B+ month; semiconductors $44.8B (+199.5% YoY), HBM broken out at $12.7B
Samsung Q2 preliminary: record ₩89.4T operating profit (19x YoY, beats ₩84.4T consensus); revenue ₩171T +129.3% — division detail comes with full results
Nanya Q2-26: GM 79.5%, record profit; guides capex toward NT$200B and capacity ~2x in 2-3 yrs — first major DDR4 supply response of the cycle

SK hynix Nasdaq ADR debut (SKHY): $26.5B — largest-ever US listing by a foreign firm, 7x oversubscribed, +13% day one; proceeds earmarked for Yongin fab, P&T7 packaging, EUV tools — the supply response is now Wall-Street-funded
BOK to parliament: chip cycle 'has yet to slow' — AI demand surging, supply expansion lagging; P10 semis exports +193% to record $11.2B (37.6% of all Korean exports)

Memory enters bear market: sector −20%+ from Jun-25 peaks, ~$1.5T semis value erased; catalysts = hynix HBM-slowdown report, Meta surplus-capacity signal, hawkish Fed, Morningstar 20-30% warning; Samsung's record Q2 sold — peak-pricing behavior while physical layer stays tight

Physical layer triple-confirms during equity bear market: Korea P20 semis +180.6% (first time >40% of all exports), Taiwan all-time record orders $95.3B (+59%, memory-attributed by MOEA), SEMI record Q1 equipment billings $36.55B — yet equipment growth of +14% vs memory revenue +100-200% quantifies the supply lag extending the squeeze
